ops.body:
prek's end-of-file-fixer (shared cc-skills precommit-base fragment, no testdata exclusion) normalizes every file to end in exactly one newline — trimming duplicates AND appending a missing one — so any on-disk golden whose byte-exact content ends otherwise cannot survive a commit-hook run; `ccx vcs ship` folds the "fix" into the commit and CI goes red on the next golden compare. Commit 4229350f's hygiene pass broke three goldens this way (fusefs note_body_delimiter.md: trailing 
 appended; internal/cli vocabulary.golden: final blank line trimmed; internal/viz graph_full.json: trailing 
 appended), and main CI stayed red from bd2d0c51 (2026-07-18T03:31) until the investigation-kind ship + its follow-up heal. The rule, now enforced by the harnesses: **every on-disk golden ends with exactly one trailing newline** — renderVocabulary trims its final blank line, normalizeGraph appends "
", and a render case that genuinely must end WITHOUT a newline is pinned as an in-code want-string test (TestNoteBodyWithoutTrailingNewline), never a golden file. Rejected alternatives: excluding testdata/golden in .pre-commit-config.yaml (cc-guides-generated; the exclusion belongs upstream in cc-skills fragments) and loader-side terminator tricks (the fixer's duplicate-trim makes any multi-newline scheme ambiguous).
